data_flow_trace.original: Governance calls StakingConfig.setUnbondingDelayMicros(type(uint64).max) at src/runtime/StakingConfig.sol:121 — only ==0 rejected, upper bound missing
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: The standalone setter setUnbondingDelayMicros no longer exists. Governance changes flow through setForNextEpoch(uint256,uint64,uint64) which calls _validateConfig. _validateConfig at L192-194 explicitly rejects _unbondingDelayMicros > MAX_UNBONDING_DELAY (=uint64(365 days)*1_000_000, ~3.15e13 μs) with Errors.ExcessiveDuration. initialize() on L86-101 also validates. A call with type(uint64).max (~1.8e19) reverts in validation.
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: requireAllowed(GOVERNANCE) plus _validateConfig MAX_UNBONDING_DELAY cap (1 year in micros) and MAX_LOCKUP_DURATION (4 years). Changes are queued in _pendingConfig and only applied by RECONFIGURATION via applyPendingConfig() at L156-171 (epoch boundary).
data_flow_trace.differs_from_original: The entire premise — a direct setUnbondingDelayMicros without upper bound — is gone. Upper bound MAX_UNBONDING_DELAY=1y was added, constant declared at L22, enforced at L192-194. Pattern changed to pending-config queued to epoch boundary.

data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: Writes are no longer immediate. setForNextEpoch stores into _pendingConfig + hasPendingConfig=true (L139-146). The live unbondingDelayMicros storage variable is only overwritten inside applyPendingConfig at L163, which is gated by requireAllowed(RECONFIGURATION). Even if governance chose a value inside [1, MAX_UNBONDING_DELAY], it does not take effect instantly.
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: RECONFIGURATION-only applyPendingConfig call at epoch transition.
data_flow_trace.differs_from_original: Immediate write path removed; replaced with pending-config/epoch-apply design.

data_flow_trace.original: Staker calls withdrawAvailable → StakePool._getClaimableAmount at src/staking/StakePool.sol:538
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: _withdrawAvailable at L615-632 calls _getClaimableAmount (L618) and returns 0 without revert when claimable==0 (L620-622). _getClaimableAmount reads unbondingDelay at L765 and early-returns 0 when now_ <= unbondingDelay (L771-773). The 'silent return 0' behavior of the withdraw path is present.
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: _pendingBuckets non-empty; now_ > unbondingDelay required to produce >0; claimedAmount bookkeeping.
data_flow_trace.differs_from_original: Line numbers moved (538 → 759-785) but semantics preserved. However the upstream attack vector (setting unbondingDelay to type(uint64).max) is no longer reachable, so the silent return path is only traversed with legitimate (bounded) delay values, not the exploit scenario.

data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: IStakingConfig(SystemAddresses.STAKE_CONFIG).unbondingDelayMicros() read at L765, returning the live, validated value (capped at MAX_UNBONDING_DELAY = 1 year).
data_flow_trace.differs_from_original: Same read call, value now provably <= 1 year due to upstream cap.

data_flow_trace.original: Early-return at line 550: if (now_ <= unbondingDelay) return 0 — true forever with absurd value
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: The early-return guard exists (L771-773) and returns 0 silently when now_ <= unbondingDelay. However, since unbondingDelay is capped at ~3.15e13 μs (~1 year), and now_ is microsecond timestamp already in ~10^15 range, this guard can only trigger extremely early in chain life, not as an exploitable stall. With 1-year cap the condition cannot be held 'forever'.
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: now_ expressed in microseconds via ITimestamp; unbondingDelay <= 1y cap.
data_flow_trace.differs_from_original: Attack required unbondingDelay near uint64.max to make condition permanent; cap makes this impossible.

data_flow_trace.original: Withdrawal returns 0 without reverting; funds locked indefinitely
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: The zero-return-without-revert pattern is still present (L620-622), but indefinite stranding from a governance-set absurd delay cannot occur because unbondingDelayMicros is validated <= 1 year at write time. Worst-case governance pressure is +1 year, not indefinite.
data_flow_trace.differs_from_original: Impact bounded to <=1 year additional delay; not 'indefinitely inaccessible' as claimed.

reasoning_chain: The issue targets setUnbondingDelayMicros at StakingConfig.sol:121 with no upper bound. In the current code at commit a623eab, that function does not exist. All governance writes to unbondingDelayMicros go through setForNextEpoch (L130) and initialize (L86), both of which call _validateConfig (L181-195). _validateConfig declares MAX_UNBONDING_DELAY=uint64(365 days)*1_000_000 (L22) and reverts with Errors.ExcessiveDuration if _unbondingDelayMicros exceeds it (L192-194). A type(uint64).max value is therefore rejected at the source. Additionally the write pattern changed to pending-config/epoch-apply, further constraining sudden changes. Downstream StakePool._getClaimableAmount still uses the silent 'return 0 when now_<=unbondingDelay' pattern (L771-773), but that path is no longer weaponizable for indefinite stranding since the delay is capped at ~1 year. Regression tests test_RevertWhen_Initialize_ExcessiveUnbondingDelay (L325-331) and the setForNextEpoch fuzz tests bounded by MAX_UNBONDING_DELAY (L286, L303) confirm the fix is covered.

key_evidence.excerpt: uint64 public constant MAX_UNBONDING_DELAY = uint64(365 days) * 1_000_000;
key_evidence.why_matters: Upper-bound constant that the original issue claims is missing.

key_evidence.excerpt: if (_unbondingDelayMicros == 0) revert Errors.InvalidUnbondingDelay(); if (_unbondingDelayMicros > MAX_UNBONDING_DELAY) { revert Errors.ExcessiveDuration(_unbondingDelayMicros, MAX_UNBONDING_DELAY); }
key_evidence.why_matters: Explicit rejection of oversize values in _validateConfig, invoked by both initialize and setForNextEpoch. Directly invalidates step 1 of the attack.

key_evidence.excerpt: function setForNextEpoch(...) external { requireAllowed(GOVERNANCE); _requireInitialized(); _validateConfig(...); _pendingConfig = PendingConfig({...}); hasPendingConfig = true; emit PendingStakingConfigSet(); }
key_evidence.why_matters: Governance path no longer writes unbondingDelayMicros directly; it queues and is applied at epoch boundary in applyPendingConfig.

key_evidence.excerpt: uint64 unbondingDelay = IStakingConfig(SystemAddresses.STAKE_CONFIG).unbondingDelayMicros(); ... if (now_ <= unbondingDelay) { return 0; } uint64 threshold = now_ - unbondingDelay;
key_evidence.why_matters: Downstream consumer still silently returns 0 on under-threshold, but value is now provably <=1 year due to upstream cap; silent-stranding premise is unreachable.

key_evidence.excerpt: uint64 maxUnbonding = config.MAX_UNBONDING_DELAY(); uint64 excessiveUnbonding = maxUnbonding + 1; vm.expectRevert(abi.encodeWithSelector(Errors.ExcessiveDuration.selector, excessiveUnbonding, maxUnbonding)); config.initialize(MIN_STAKE, LOCKUP_DURATION, excessiveUnbonding);
key_evidence.why_matters: Regression test directly asserting the cap is enforced.

counterargument: One could argue the silent-return-0 behavior in StakePool._getClaimableAmount (L620-622, L771-773) is a latent design smell worth fixing regardless of the upstream cap, since a conservative governance delay set near MAX_UNBONDING_DELAY (1 year) still defers withdrawals for up to one year without any revert or user-visible event. Additionally, the pending-config pattern means a bad proposal inside the valid range could still be scheduled at an epoch boundary; observability remains degraded. However, the specific vulnerability filed — unbounded governance-set delay producing 'indefinite' silent stranding — is defeated by MAX_UNBONDING_DELAY=1 year. The residual concern is severity-low UX/observability, not the Medium-severity silent-stranding attack claimed.
